title: JavaScript Variables Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of the essential properties and tools for creating robust and cross-browser web experiences with variables and data types.

questions.question: Which JavaScript keyword should you use specifically to define a 'Constant' value (a value that will NEVER change)?
Which 'Pillar of the Web' is responsible for providing behavior and interactivity to a page (e.g., using variables to store user scores)?
JavaScript is 'Dynamically Typed,' which means you DO NOT have to tell the variable what kind of data it will hold ahead of time. True or False?
One of the most powerful use cases for 'Variables' is ________ (naming them in camelCase like totalProjectCost).
What is the primary danger of using the older 'var' keyword instead of 'let' or 'const' in your modern project?
options: It can cause 'scoping' issues and unexpected behavior in complex applications.
It is not supported in modern browsers.
